channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: How the Gilgo Beach Killings Fueled a Mania for Prestige True Crime
item.description: With its lurid details and eerily desolate crime scene, the Long Island serial killer case invited rampant speculation. The reality was more mundane, and much worse.
item.dc:creator: Ginia Bellafante
item.category: Lost Girls: An Unsolved American Mystery (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Johnny Milano for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: After more than a decade with no suspect in the Gilgo Beach killings, a Long Island architect, Rex Heuermann, was arrested in 2023.

item.title: Shannan Gilbert’s Death Remains the Great Unsolved Mystery of the Gilgo Beach Murders
item.description: The death of 23-year-old Shannan Gilbert remains the great unsolved mystery surrounding the Gilgo Beach serial killer case.
item.dc:creator: Robert Kolker

media:content.media:credit: Kevin Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mari Gilbert at the burial of her daughter Shannan Gilbert. Shannan’s disappearance led to the investigation of a serial killer; yet her death was never considered a murder.

item.title: De repente arrastraba los pies al caminar. ¿Por qué?
item.description: La hidrocefalia normotensiva puede manifestarse con incontinencia urinaria, demencia o cambios en la forma de caminar y afecta a más de uno de cada 20 adultos mayores de 80 años.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Sanders, M.D.

item.title: ‘I Saw a Buzzard on a Dead Tree Branch No More Than 20 Feet Away’
item.description: A bird of prey in Morningside Park, reflections on the urban bustle and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 12, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Fail to Agree on Peace Deal, Vance Says, Leaving Cease-Fire’s Fate Uncertain
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said the Iranian delegation had not accepted American terms for ending the war after a marathon session in Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: La inteligencia de EE. UU. muestra que China tiene un papel más activo en la guerra de Irán
item.description: China podría haber enviado misiles a Irán, y Pekín está permitiendo que empresas vendan a Teherán suministros que pueden usarse en la producción militar, según funcionarios estadounidenses.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una valla publicitaria antiestadounidense el domingo en Teherán que muestra aviones estadounidenses capturados en una red.

item.title: Trump’s War With Iran Has Weakened America
item.description: The president should at long last recognize the ineptitude of his impulsive, go-it-alone approach.
item.dc:creator: The Editorial Board
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Jason Hendardy for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Vivir más y mejor? Es lo que estudia la medicina de la longevidad
item.description: Este campo está lleno de oportunidades y charlatanería. He aquí cómo navegarlo según expertos.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: A New Era of World War Has Arrived
item.description: As the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East drag on, the world war is returning.
item.dc:creator: Paul Poast
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Parents, Consider Underachieving
item.description: I am just so tired, and so bad at crafts.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Feintzeig

item.title: TK
item.description: TK

item.title: Control of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s Uranium Stockpiles Were Sticking Points
item.description: The U.S. had demanded that Iran immediately reopen the strait to all maritime traffic, but Iran said it would do so only after a final peace deal, according to Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi

media:content.media:description: Journalists broadcast inside the Jinnah Convention Centre, where media gathered to cover talks between U.S and Iranian officials taking place at the nearby Serena Hotel in Islamabad, Pakistan on Saturday.

item.title: Mass Stampede at Haitian Tourist Site Leaves Dozens Dead
item.description: The tragedy took place at the Citadelle Laferrière, a historic fortress in northern Haiti and one of the country’s defining symbols.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre and Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:description: The Citadelle Laferrière, a large 19th-century fortress in northern Haiti, in 2024.

item.title: What Now? Vance Leaves Iran Talks Without a Deal.
item.description: The lack of a breakthrough after 21 hours of negotiations leaves the Trump administration facing several unpalatable options.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance leaving after negotiations with Iran in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday.

item.title: What to Know as Hungary Votes in Elections Watched by the World
item.description: With Prime Minister Viktor Orban, a standard-bearer for global populism, in political peril, the vote on Sunday could have far-reaching implications.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins

media:content.media:description: Viktor Orban, Hungary’s prime minister of 16 years, campaigning in Szekesfehervar, Hungary, on Friday.

item.title: Many Polls Say Orban Will Lose. But He Has an Edge Even Before Voting Begins.
item.description: Over 16 years, Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party has repeatedly tweaked the electoral system to its advantage, making the vote free, but not entirely fair.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai

media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary addressing his supporters during an election rally on Friday.

item.title: Book Review: ‘Passport Photo Service,’ by Philip Sharkey
item.description: In 10 minutes or less, this mom-and-pop London institution produced stylish snapshots for some of the world’s biggest stars.
item.dc:creator: Erica Ackerberg
item.category: Passport Photo Service: An Unexpected Archive of Celebrity Portraits (Book)

item.title: Jailed for Love Songs? Yes, and Still Singing.
item.description: Nguyen Van Loc was persecuted for performing Hanoi’s old romantic music during (and after) the Vietnam War. He can’t stop. He won’t stop.
item.dc:creator: Damien Cave and Linh Pham

media:content.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times

item.title: Want to Star Opposite Daniel Radcliffe? At ‘Every Brilliant Thing,’ You Have a Chance.
item.description: Before each performance, the actor sprints around the Hudson Theater enlisting audience members to take part in the interactive show.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Amir Hamja

media:content.media:description: “The engine that powers this show is kindness,” Daniel Radcliffe said. “If you get up and you’re kind, everything else is a bonus.”

item.title: Corrections: April 12, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, April 12, 2026.

item.title: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
item.description: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump, left, sat ringside at a U.F.C. event in Miami on Saturday. His son Donald Trump Jr. is at right.

item.title: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes
item.description: The House could move within days to consider expelling Representative Eric Swalwell, Democrat of California, in light of sexual assault allegations against him. There could be more to come for both parties.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni

media:content.media:description: Four House members could face expulsion in the coming weeks.

item.title: Second Venezuelan Doctor Detained in South Texas by Immigration Agents
item.description: An E.R. doctor was detained Saturday, just days after a family physician had been detained. Both were traveling when immigration agents took them into custody.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

item.title: U.S. and Iran Hold Peace Talks in Pakistan
item.description: At a historic meeting in Islamabad, Vice President JD Vance and senior Iranian officials were seeking to make a fragile truce permanent.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:description: Vice President JD Vance being greeted on Saturday by Pakistani officials in Islamabad, Pakistan.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Astronauts Reunite With Friends and Family After 10-Day Moon Mission
item.description: The four astronauts made an emotional return to Houston a day after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean at the end of their 10-day lunar journey.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

item.title: Southwest Airlines Imposes Further Limits on Portable Chargers on Flights
item.description: Starting on April 20, the airline will limit passengers to one lithium battery-powered portable charger per person.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Southwest Airlines said that starting April 20, passengers will be allowed one lithium-battery charger per person aboard their flights.

item.title: Judges Fired After Blocking Deportations of Pro-Palestinian Students
item.description: The immigration judges’ abrupt dismissals marked the latest efforts by the Trump administration to reshape the country’s immigration courts.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Nicholas Nehamas and Steven Rich

media:content.media:description: The Justice Department has dismissed dozens of immigration judges and, according to those on the bench, has put judges under pressure to deny asylum claims and order deportations.

item.title: Initial Thoughts
item.description: Lance Enfinger and John Kugelman team up for an extended challenge that’s full of abbreviations.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: A solver, maybe, getting a bit roughed up on “Game of Thrones” day at the ballpark.

item.title: Police Fatally Shoot Man After Stabbings at Grand Central Subway Station
item.description: A man with a large knife was shot by police after stabbing three people at Grand Central subway station on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Heather Khalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A member of the Police Department’s Crime Scene Unit was on the scene after stabbings and a shooting occurred at the Grand Central subway station.

item.title: Someone Has to Be Happy. Why Not Lauren Sánchez Bezos?
item.description: As half of an unfathomably powerful couple, Mrs. Sánchez Bezos seems to have influenced the uber-rich to stop apologizing, and start enjoying themselves.
item.dc:creator: Amy Chozick

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lauren Sánchez Bezos treats the pursuit, and spreading, of joy as a kind of mandate.

item.title: 3 California Men Are Arrested in $1 Million Lego Heist
item.description: Sheriff’s deputies spotted the men fleeing in two box trucks on Wednesday. Deputies searching the cargo discovered a large amount of Lego products, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz

item.title: Forest Hills Homeowners Win $150,000 in Battle Over Concert Noise
item.description: The Queens residents have long complained that concerts at nearby Forest Hills Stadium are degrading their quality of life. Last month, they won a settlement from the city in a related dispute.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Ahmed Gaber for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Forest Hills Stadium hosts some high-decibel concerts.

item.title: Takeaways From the First Big Gathering of Potential 2028 Democratic Candidates
item.description: The National Action Network convention showcased similarities and some differences in a wide-open field of possible contenders.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, embracing the Rev. Al Sharpton, received a raucous response from the crowd at the National Action Network.

item.title: Navy Warships Cross Strait of Hormuz to Clear Mines, U.S. Says
item.description: Iran denied that the American destroyers had entered the strait, as negotiations for an extended cease-fire continued in Islamabad.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Julian E. Barnes, Adam Rasgon and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A bulk carrier ship near the Strait of Hormuz last month in northern Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Appeals Court Allows Work on Trump’s Ballroom Project for Now, but Seeks Clarity on Scope
item.description: A three-judge panel allowed construction on the ballroom to proceed until April 17, but asked a lower court to untangle the details of the president’s plans.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Planning Commission voted this month to approve Mr. Trump’s ballroom project.

item.title: David Sklansky, ‘Mathematician’ of High-Stakes Poker, Dies at 78
item.description: As “the first nerd to enter poker,” he helped transform a game long ruled by intuition into one based on game theory, probability and logic.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

item.title: The Athletic Investigates Conduct of Reporter Photographed With N.F.L. Coach
item.description: Photographs of the reporter, Dianna Russini, and Mike Vrabel of the New England Patriots appeared in The New York Post.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson

media:content.media:description: Dianna Russini, a senior N.F.L. reporter for The Athletic, before the start of a football game in 2020.

item.title: Manhattan DA Opens Investigation Into Eric Swalwell After Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: The Manhattan district attorney’s office is examining a claim that Representative Eric Swalwell, a candidate for governor in California, assaulted a woman in New York City in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: At least four women accuse Eric Swalwell of sexual misconduct.

item.title: USPS Is Running Out of Money. Here’s How It Could Affect Your Mail.
item.description: Despite numerous attempts to reform the Postal Service, the agency’s business model has not changed significantly since 1970. Officials are proposing to decrease service and increase prices.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: On Thursday, the Postal Service proposed increasing the cost of stamps by 5 percent, and said that it would temporarily suspend some payments to a government retirement fund.

item.title: Postal Service, Under Pressure, Now Faces Trump’s Mail Ballot Order
item.description: President Trump’s executive order, which has been challenged as unconstitutional, would limit the Postal Service to sending only the ballots of voters deemed eligible.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump’s executive order on mail ballots is one of many financial and logistical challenges facing the Postal Service.

item.title: Artemis II nos mostró nuevas imágenes de la Luna
item.description: Durante 10 días, los astronautas de la misión de sobrevuelo lunar de la NASA han logrado algo casi imposible.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Moves to Deport Son of Prominent Figure in Iranian Revolution
item.description: Federal agents arrested a man whose mother served as a spokeswoman for the Islamist embassy captors during the hostage crisis that began in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The Trump administration announced plans to deport the son of Masoumeh Ebtekar, the former vice president of Iran, seen here during a news conference in 2019.

item.title: Pentagon Asks Court to Keep Its Restrictions on Journalists
item.description: The Defense Department wants to keep in place a policy requiring escorts for journalists in the building while it appeals a court decision that tossed out broader restrictions on the media.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: “Allowing members of the public unfettered access to the Pentagon poses unique and acute dangers,” the Pentagon said in a court filing.

item.title: Pearl Fryar, a Picasso of Plants, Dies at 86
item.description: A self-taught topiary artist, he discovered a talent for carving trees and shrubs into extraordinary shapes, creating a world-famous garden in a tiny South Carolina town.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

media:content.media:credit: Imke Lass for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mr. Fryar in 2005. He began landscaping his yard more than four decades ago in an effort to win an award from the local garden club.

item.title: U.S.-Iran Talks Timeline: Key Moments Between Two Adversaries
item.description: From sanctions relief to nuclear talks, here’s a look at how negotiations between Washington and Tehran have gone over the decades.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, on Friday as the city prepares to host the talks between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Officer Fatally Shoots Attacker at Grand Central After 3 Are Stabbed With Machete
item.description: The victims, two older men and an older woman, were expected to survive after the subway platform attacks. The assailant was shot after he repeatedly refused to drop his weapon, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Heather Khalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A member of the Police Department’s Crime Scene Unit was on the scene after stabbings and a shooting occurred at the Grand Central subway station.

item.title: The Tester Called This ‘a Perfect Recipe’
item.description: Meet Ali Slagle’s new cottage cheese basil pasta.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Ali Slagle’s creamy cottage cheese basil pasta.
